| [0003](./0003/) | cli | draft | - | partial | 2026-07-20 — Rescoped to cover version agreement, and refreshed against what enhancement 0006 landed in the meantime. D1's mapping is no longer a proposal — it shipped in 0006's C1 slice as cli/pkg/module/module.go CanonicalModuleRef, but in its weakest form: the consumer derives and nothing binds, with no publish enforcement and no library helper (implementation.status not-started -> partial). NEW SCOPE: metadata.version must equal the release tag of the artifact carrying it (D3). This was previously unaddressed and is the more dangerous half — the addressing drift fails loudly as 'module not found', whereas a stale version fails silently: the CLI deploys the artifact it fetched but records a different version in spec.module.version, so after a handoff the operator reconciles a DIFFERENT artifact indefinitely with Ready=True and every gate green. metadata.version is load-bearing in three places (fqn -> module.uuid -> instance.uuid -> the identity label on every rendered resource; spec.module.version; and synth/render.go:62's major() for the synthesized import), so none of them are sound while it may lie. Publishing today is raw `cue mod publish` with the tag read from a separate versions.yml in the modules repo — a third source of truth, and there is no `opm module publish` at all. NEW DECISIONS: D3 (version agreement, expressed in schemas/target.cue as #PublishedModuleRef, which unifies the derived depVersion with the artifact coordinates in hand so a mismatch is a type error — verified: a mismatched example yields `conflicting values "v0.1.3" and "v0.2.0"`); D4 (publish DERIVES coordinates from metadata, never STAMPS them into the artifact — stamping would make published bytes differ from source, breaking reproducibility and the local-vs-registry byte identity that 0006's render-parity D30 proves and the handoff digest gate rests on); D5 (closes OQ2 — a bare import binds, verified empirically via 0006 C2's fixture work rather than assumed); D6 (closes OQ5 — the invariant is enforced at ACQUIRE in library, not only at publish, because `cue mod publish` exists and every module published to date bypassed OPM tooling; placing the check on kernel.AcquireModuleFromRegistry means the CLI and operator inherit it from one implementation). This inverts 02-design's centre of gravity: publish-side derivation is the ergonomic half, acquire-side verification is the guarantee. NEW OPEN QUESTIONS: OQ6 (what a --version override means under D4 — assertion / rewrite-source-then-publish / tag-only, the last ruled out as reintroducing exactly the drift D3 removes) and OQ7 (whether #Catalog's identical exposure — version! with a *"0.0.0-dev" default, feeding catalog FQNs and therefore transformer matching — is specified here and implemented in 0001, or moves wholesale). EVIDENCE recorded in 01-problem.md: this enhancement's own target.cue always specified `depVersion: "v" + version`, yet the C1 implementation returned metadata.version verbatim; CUE rejects a bare version and the operator passes spec.module.version straight to the registry loader, so EVERY CLI-written ModuleInstance was unresolvable by the operator until 2026-07-20. It survived because the CLI never reads that field back, the operator was only tested against hand-written YAML fixtures, and the unit test asserted the bare form as expected — a mapping that is merely derivable-by-convention drifts from its implementations. | OPM Module Publishing Workflow |
| [0004](./0004/) | cross-cutting | accepted | none | not-started | 2026-06-18 — Accepted | Automated CUE Dependency Updates via Dagger |
| [0005](./0005/) | cross-cutting | draft | - | not-started | 2026-06-20 — Design direction set from research synthesis — Model B (sugar composes onto generated mirror) chosen; single cross-cutting umbrella; core change ruled out as not required | Kubernetes-Native Refocus: Generated Mirror and Composed Abstractions |
| [0006](./0006/) | cli | accepted | none | in-progress | 2026-07-20 — C3 adoption path VERIFIED END-TO-END — tasks 5.2/5.3 closed, and two further cross-actor bugs found by running it. A reconciling operator now runs in kind-opm-dev via a new `task cluster:operator` (cli/Taskfile.yml): connect the opm-registry container to kind's docker network, `opm operator install`, patch the controller Deployment's args with `--registry=opmodel.dev=opm-registry:5000+insecure,testing.opmodel.dev=...` (the operator's registry is a FLAG — OPM_REGISTRY is only read when the flag is empty and it defaults to GHCR, and the operator overwrites CUE_REGISTRY in its own env, so pod env vars do nothing), apply the cluster Platform singleton (hack/kind-platform.yaml, ranges mirroring the CLI's DefaultPlatformTemplate so both actors materialize the same catalogs), and wait for status.operatorVersion. Blueprint was opm-operator's own .tasks/kind.yaml, not opm-kind-demo (whose operator uses public GHCR and whose local registry only holds a Flux bundle). Supporting files: hack/opm-config.cue (hermetic dev config via --config, so the tooling does not depend on a developer's ~/.opm), hack/kind-operator-rbac.yaml (DEV-ONLY: the shipped manager role grants the operator NO workload permissions — by design it impersonates a per-instance ServiceAccount named in spec.serviceAccountName, a field the CLI does not write, so a CLI-created instance has no applier identity and every operator apply 403s), and the first renderable instance fixture in cli/ (tests/e2e/testdata/handoff/, core@v1 + podinfo@v0 v0.1.3). RETRACTION: the earlier claim that the pinned operator image was unpullable was never verified and was wrong — the image was already in the kind node; only `--crds-only` had ever been run, so the controller Deployment simply never existed. BUG 1 (cross-actor contract, C1 origin): pkg/module CanonicalModuleRef returned a BARE semver ('0.1.3') for spec.module.version, but the operator passes that field straight to the registry loader with no normalization and CUE rejects a bare version as malformed — so EVERY CLI-written ModuleInstance was unresolvable by the operator, defeating the shared-CR premise. The operator's own CRD documents the v-prefixed form ('Example: "v0.2.1"') and its podinfo fixture uses v0.1.2. Fixed to emit 'v0.1.3'; majorVersionTag also hardened against a v-prefixed input (it produced 'vv0'), caught by the idempotency case added alongside. BUG 2 (C3 origin, honesty): operator-owned delete reported 'operator pruned N resources' after merely observing the CR disappear. spec.prune has no CRD default and the CLI never writes it, so the operator logs 'Prune disabled, orphaning managed resources on deletion' and leaves every workload running — the CLI was reporting a cleanup that provably had not happened. Record now carries spec.prune and delete reports the actual outcome, naming the kubectl patch that enables pruning. Both delete outcomes are e2e-covered. VERIFIED LIVE: handoff adoption (verification render matches, 2 resources adopted, Ready=True, inventory entry set unchanged, revision incremented, POD UIDs IDENTICAL before/after, managed-by relabelled opm-cli -> opm-controller — D40's 'relabel only, no workload changes' is now an observation, not a design claim); digest mismatch aborts showing both digests with ownership untouched, and --force overrides exactly that gate; thin-editor values round-trip (spec.values updated, spec.owner preserved, operator scaled the Deployment); operator-owned delete in both prune modes; all four precondition refusals. e2e gating is hard-fail (not skip) per user decision, with the destructive operator-lifecycle test now rebuilding the dev operator in a LIFO t.Cleanup so a full suite run stays re-runnable — proven by two consecutive green full runs (~160s each). Lint sits at 86, one below the pre-existing 87 baseline. | CLI CR Inventory, Library Kernel Adoption, and Operator Handoff |

Two scope questions seeded as Open Questions (kustomize-build vs raw-only; relationship to 0005) | Manifest Passthrough: Side-Channel Raw and Kustomize Manifests |

## Status legend

- **draft** — initial design, actively being written
- **accepted** — design agreed upon, ready for implementation
- **implemented** — design has been realized in code
- **superseded** — replaced by a newer enhancement (see `superseded_by` in the entry's config.yaml)
